seoTitle: How to Create a Carousel Effect in Canva
metaDescription: Learn how to create a carousel effect animation in Canva to showcase multiple images or slides with smooth transitions.
intro: A carousel-style animation lets you present multiple images or ideas in sequence, keeping viewers engaged as content transitions smoothly across the screen. This is popular for product showcases and social media promotions. This guide covers setting one up in Canva.

steps.heading: Set up a video or multi-page design
steps.body: Create a Canva video project, or a design with multiple pages that will act as your carousel slides.

steps.heading: Arrange your images or content in order
steps.body: Place each image or slide in the sequence you want it to appear during the carousel effect.

steps.heading: Apply page or element transitions
steps.body: Use Canva's transition options between pages, or animate elements to slide in and out.

steps.heading: Adjust timing between slides
steps.body: Set how long each image displays before transitioning to the next for a smooth, readable pace.

steps.heading: Preview and export the animation
steps.body: Play through the full sequence to check the timing, then export as a video or share it as an interactive link.

faq.question: Can I use the carousel effect for a static social media post?
faq.answer: For platforms like Instagram, you'd typically export separate images for a native carousel post rather than a single animated file.

faq.question: Are transitions available on Canva's free plan?
faq.answer: Yes, a range of transitions and animations are included for free, with more premium options in Canva Pro.

faq.question: How many slides can I include in a carousel animation?
faq.answer: There's no strict limit, but keeping it concise usually holds viewer attention better.

faq.question: Can I add music to a carousel-style video?
faq.answer: Yes, you can layer background music or a voiceover alongside the visual transitions.
